what_it_does: Records an image attachment entry for the design-assets channel, linking to a specific image asset target and timestamping when it was added.
observations: File contains only YAML frontmatter with no additional body content
standouts: Target filename includes a '(1)' suffix, suggesting a duplicate or downloaded copy
Channel is design-assets, indicating a design-system asset
conventions: Includes id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added fields
Timestamp follows ISO 8601 with timezone offset
Target uses an image:// URI scheme
concerns: The '(1)' suffix in the target filename may indicate an accidental duplicate or unrenamed export
No caption, title, or description is provided for the image
It is unclear whether the target URI is resolvable from this file alone
notes: A minimal attachment ledger entry that registers a white circular icon concept image in the design-assets channel.
questions: Does the '(1)' suffix denote a version, a duplicate, or an auto-renamed download?
Who added this attachment and for what purpose?
Is the image:// target resolvable and what are its dimensions/format?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to record an attachment, and the file does exactly that.
intention_signals: To create a timestamped, typed record associating a channel with a specific image asset for later reference and audit.
contribution: Provides a timestamped, typed link between a channel and a specific image asset, supporting asset discovery, audit trails, and channel-based browsing.
summary_for_embedding: Attachment metadata record att-fdf33303 for the design-assets channel pointing to the image asset Icon_ConceptV_VCircle_White (1).
intention_for_embedding: To log an image attachment in the design-assets channel with a stable target reference and creation timestamp.
